title: Sakura Nagashi or Beautiful World
wikitext:
[]: Sakura Nagashi or Beautiful World?             Quote Post by WunderBah » Fri Aug 30, 2013 11:47 pm Which song did you felt was better overall? For me, i honestly can't choose since both are amazing and fitting for Rebuild IMO (LOVED the acoustic version of it in 2.0's ending credits). But if i had to, Sakura Nagashi gets a little bit of the edge for me.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Aug 31, 2013 12:41 pm Beautiful World, specifically the version used in 2.22. To be fair, I readily admit I'm incapable of judging the songs on their own merits due to how deep the association they have with their respective movies runs. Regardless, even without 3.0, I feel like Sakura Nagashi has a kind of sadness to it. Perhaps I'm misjudging it but I think, were I prone to tear up at music, it would be a song that made me cry. It's definitely beautiful, but the slower tone also makes it kind of sad. Beautiful World is more upbeat, and I tend to prefer upbeat music. I'm also very fond of the lyrics of BW (well, the translated lyrics of course). To my ears alone I don't think BW is as beautiful as SN, but knowing the lyrics I think it is.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Aug 31, 2013 12:46 pm CJD wrote: Perhaps I'm misjudging it but I think, were I prone to tear up at music, it would be a song that made me cry. It's definitely beautiful, but the slower tone also makes it kind of sad.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Aug 31, 2013 12:59 pm Sakura Nagashi . Although the words are somewhat upbeat ("everybody finds love, in the end " - but when...?), the whole effect is of overwhelming melancholy, and I like that.

[]: Re: Sakura Nagashi or Beautiful World?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Aug 31, 2013 1:37 pm WunderBah wrote: Which song did you felt was better overall?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Aug 31, 2013 2:19 pm Bagheera wrote: What a peculiar question. Why compare them in such a fashion to begin with?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Aug 31, 2013 3:23 pm But not everyone does find love, and it does not seem likely that anyone in the Q cast will

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Aug 31, 2013 3:32 pm pwhodges wrote: A light-hearted question - could be in Chatter, but is Rebuild specific, so...

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Aug 31, 2013 3:36 pm Bagheera wrote: If asked which I prefer, though, that's another matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Aug 31, 2013 3:39 pm pwhodges wrote: I'd say that "which is better" is a perfectly idiomatic, if not accurate way of asking that. And a musician could answer in yet another way, comparing the musical construction and its relationship to their affect ( sic ) in each case.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Aug 31, 2013 3:42 pm I've always loved Beautiful World (the original mix), but Sakura Nagashi just has more impact on me. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Conspicuous » Sat Aug 31, 2013 4:28 pm Stillborn wrote: That's why the words that everyone finds love in the end, are bullshit to me. Especially in context of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by kboyrulez12345 » Sat Aug 31, 2013 6:28 pm I agree with Bagheera on this one. Both songs are for different purposes, sort of like the apples and oranges comparison. Sakura Nagashi is a song about how there will always be hope in the end(though according to Utada, it's about dealing being a single mom.) Beautiful World is about a song about trying to love yourself(I think. I haven't watched Eva in a while.) Basically there two different spectrum. Though, in accordance with sound, I like Sakura Nagashi more. I like the type of songs that are slow and somber for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Sep 01, 2013 7:39 am Thread surgery complete. The tangent did not survive the operation -- except for this part of a post: FreakyFilmFan4Ever wrote: Anyways, I really can't tell which song I would favor over the other. I'm tempted to go with Sakura Nagashi because grief tends to be more memorable than joy. People can easily forget the truly good things that happen to them all the time, but that bad day someone could have at work still pops up every now and again.

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Sun Sep 01, 2013 7:55 am Anyone listen to the instrumental version of Sakura Nagashi? Pure piano bliss to me. If J-Pop ever gets tiring, I recommend this song's instrumentals. Coincidentally, I like me some Chopin. I'd say melody-wise, SN is more evocative than BW. Something about its simple arrangement reminds me of the same feeling I get from listening to Fur Elise

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Sep 01, 2013 8:06 am Somehow, I find pain more memorable and more 'real' than joy, and joy as something transcient, temporary. Perhaps it's a result of our mental states - someone in constant pain of fear and suffering and seeking to avoid it tends to remember suffering, and someone seeking joy tends to remember that. Mono no aware or a 'pathos towards things' is very much what dictates Sakura Nagashi, down to the Sakura. The Eva pilots are permanent, the world around them is impermanent. The true curse of Eva is permanence . If I were to choose one over the other, I suppose it would depend on what I want to hear at the moment - but Sakura Nagashi has far more impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Mon Sep 02, 2013 12:57 pm I like Beautiful World, and I love the 2.22 version. But I love Sakura Nagashi even more. Though admittedly I'm a sucker for melancholy music.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Tue Sep 03, 2013 3:29 am Fly me to the moon. but srs here, not a huge fan of beautiful world, so Sakura for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Amitch1991 » Tue Sep 03, 2013 11:37 am Sakura Nagashi for me :) although I like both, Sakura is a little better for me

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Sep 07, 2013 8:57 am As it has been mentioned upthread, comparing the two feels a bit like comparing apples and oranges since they seem to serve very different purposes. That said, i vastly prefer Sakura Nagashi over Beautiful World. I love Beautiful World too and it's one of the most inspiring and lively songs i've heard, but overall Sakura Nagashi has this almost spiritual sort of beauty and happiness to it which makes it for me a much "higher" song than Beautiful World. I guess if i had to put it in a way, i find the beauty and happiness in Sakura much more meaningful and absolute than the joy Beautiful World brings me. Musically, i'm not sure if i can decide on which one i prefer since they're both really great songs.
summary:
[]: Sakura Nagashi or Beautiful World?             Quote Post by WunderBah » Fri Aug 30, 2013 11:47 pm Which song did you felt was better overall? For me, i honestly can't choose since both are amazing and fitting for Rebuild IMO (LOVED the acoustic version of it in 2.0's ending credits). But if i had to, Sakura Nagashi gets a little bit of the edge for me.

[]:             Q
